subject: Biology
title: Allele Frequencies: Evolution's Scoreboard
description: A 60-second lesson on Allele Frequencies: Evolution's Scoreboard.

hook.text: Evolution isn't about individuals changing. It's about populations shifting—one allele at a time.

buildup.text: An allele is a version of a gene. Every population carries a mix of alleles at different frequencies.

discovery.text: Evolution occurs when allele frequencies change over generations—through selection, drift, or migration.

twist.text: A trait can be beneficial yet decrease in a small population—luck sometimes overrides fitness.

climax.text: Population genetics unifies Darwin's ideas with Mendel's genetics into the modern synthesis.

punchline.text: Evolution is a numbers game played with genes.

quiz.question: When does evolution occur in a population?
options: When allele frequencies change across generations
